notes.note: Cephalio was one of Atticus's regular letter-carriers (tabellarii); his arrival from Rome with a more cheerful despatch is set against the gloomier news Cicero had just relayed from Capua.

notes.anchor: He carried his bad causes through always; in the best of causes he has collapsed
notes.note: Cicero's verdict on Pompey: he had prevailed in his earlier, dubious enterprises but failed now in the cause of the Republic, lacking the difficult art of governing the state rightly.
